name: quality
description: Code quality validation, formatting, linting, and pre-commit checks.
qualityReason: Good but overlaps with other quality skills
capabilities: Format Python code with Black and Ruff
Run linting checks with Ruff PyStrict rules
Perform type checking with BasedPyright strict mode
Apply blind except and error message best practices
author: ByronWilliamsCPA
securitySummary: All 14 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. This is a documentation-only skill containing SKILL.md (documentation) and skill-report.json (metadata). No executable code, scripts, or commands exist. Static patterns triggered on common English words (validation, check, mode, apply) without cryptographic meaning. The source URL, model name, and directory paths are legitimate metadata, not security threats.
valueStatement: Ensure consistent code quality across Python projects. This skill automates formatting with Black, linting with Ruff, and type checking with BasedPyright to maintain professional code standards.
limitations: Requires Python project with uv package manager
Does not fix issues automatically without user approval
Assumes pre-commit configuration is already set up

useCases.title: Enforce Code Standards
useCases.description: Maintain consistent formatting and linting across team Python projects with automated quality checks

useCases.title: Pre-commit Validation
useCases.description: Run comprehensive pre-commit hooks to catch issues before code is committed to version control

useCases.title: Automated Quality Gates
useCases.description: Verify code meets quality standards using BasedPyright type checking and Ruff linting rules

promptTemplates.title: Quick Format
promptTemplates.prompt: Format this Python code with Black and Ruff

promptTemplates.title: Full Lint
promptTemplates.prompt: Run ruff check with all rules and fix any issues found

promptTemplates.title: Type Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Run basedpyright on the src directory and report any type errors

promptTemplates.title: Pre-commit Run
promptTemplates.prompt: Run pre-commit hooks on all files to ensure they pass quality checks

bestPractices: Run quality checks before committing code to catch issues early
Use BasedPyright strict mode to catch type errors at development time
Configure pre-commit hooks to automate quality checks on every commit
antiPatterns: Skipping linting checks because they seem overly strict
Ignoring type checker warnings without understanding them
Running pre-commit hooks only after noticing a CI failure

faq.question: Does this skill support languages other than Python?
faq.answer: No, this skill is specifically designed for Python projects using Black, Ruff, and BasedPyright.

faq.question: What happens if pre-commit hooks fail?
faq.answer: The skill reports which hooks failed and why. You must fix the issues before committing.

faq.question: Can I customize the linting rules?
faq.answer: Yes, modify your pyproject.toml to adjust Ruff rules. This skill uses PyStrict defaults.

faq.question: Is my code sent to external servers?
faq.answer: No. All tools run locally on your machine. No code leaves your development environment.

faq.question: Why are some type errors ignored in tests?
faq.answer: Tests may have relaxed rules for pragmatic development. Adjust per-file ignores in pyproject.toml.

faq.question: How is this different from running tools manually?
faq.answer: This skill provides a unified interface and guides you through the complete quality workflow.
